---
title: "Homework Assignments"
subtitle: "All ten assignments from Math 3190: Fundamentals of Data Science"
---

This page collects every homework assignment from the course. Click any card below to open the full write-up with code, output, and answers. You can also use the sidebar on the left to jump between assignments.

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 1](Homework_1.qmd)
*Due Jan 29, 2026* &middot; Notes 1&ndash;3

Setting up the data science toolkit: navigating the Unix command line, version-controlling a project with Git and GitHub, wrangling data with the `tidyverse` and pipes, and writing a first reproducible Quarto document with R, LaTeX, and Python code chunks.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 2](Homework_2.qmd)
*Due Feb 5, 2026* &middot; Notes 4

Querying relational databases from R. Writing SQL (`SELECT`, `WHERE`, `GROUP BY`, joins) against a local SQLite database using `DBI` and `RSQLite`, and translating `dplyr` pipelines into SQL with `dbplyr`.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 3](Homework_3.qmd)
*Due Feb 12, 2026* &middot; Notes 5

Verification methods for classifiers and regressors &mdash; training/test splits, confusion matrices, sensitivity and specificity, $k$-nearest neighbors, picking $k$, and $k$-fold cross-validation tuned through `caret`.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 4](Homework_4.qmd)
*Due Feb 19, 2026* &middot; Notes 6

Optimization for statistics. Implementing Newton's method and gradient descent by hand, then using R's `optimize()` and `optim()` to fit models via Maximum Likelihood Estimation.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 5](Homework_5.qmd)
*Due Feb 26, 2026* &middot; Notes 5 & 7 (Part 1)

Bootstrap resampling for inference paired with a linear-regression refresher: checking model diagnostics and using remedial measures like transformations, bootstrapped confidence intervals, and weighted least squares.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 6](Homework_6.qmd)
*Due Mar 5, 2026* &middot; Notes 7 (Part 2)

Advanced regression: ridge, LASSO, and elastic-net regularization via `glmnet`, the bias&ndash;variance trade-off, logistic-regression elastic nets, and Poisson / quasi-Poisson models for count data.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 7](Homework_7.qmd)
*Due Mar 26, 2026* &middot; Notes 8

Dimension reduction for modeling and visualization &mdash; principal component analysis (PCA), partial least squares regression with interpretable PLS surrogates, and the non-linear UMAP projection.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 8](Homework_8.qmd)
*Due Apr 2, 2026* &middot; Notes 9

Bayesian methods: conditional probability and Bayes' theorem, deriving priors, likelihoods, and posteriors, and drawing from posteriors via Gibbs and Metropolis MCMC samplers (with diagnostics).
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 9](Homework_9.qmd)
*Due Apr 9, 2026* &middot; Notes 10

More classification techniques &mdash; the Naïve Bayes classifier, Linear Discriminant Analysis (LDA), and hard- and soft-margin Support Vector Machines for both linear and non-linear decision boundaries.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6 .g-col-lg-4}
### [Homework 10](Homework_10.qmd)
*Due Apr 16, 2026* &middot; Notes 11

Time series analysis. Exploring autocorrelation (ACF/PACF), decomposing a series into trend, seasonality, and error, fitting MA, AR, ARMA, and ARIMA models, and producing forecasts with `auto.arima()`.
:::

:::
